prompt: Camera move left, pull out, then pan left. A high alpine valley in late autumn opens from a foreground of frost-stiff sedges and dark glacial stones to a braided river winding toward distant snow-striped peaks. A low storm is breaking apart after dawn; ragged cloud bands drag across the mountain shoulders while a colder blue sky appears in gaps overhead. On one side, a spruce forest climbs a moraine in dense stepped layers, and on the other, ochre tundra terraces descend toward wet gravel bars. Meltwater channels glint unevenly, puddles shiver under gusts, and thin veils of mist lift from the river as pale sunlight gradually touches the ridgelines.

Camera move right, pull out, then pan right. An eroded desert badlands basin stretches across stacked distances, beginning with cracked clay and angular talus, then descending into labyrinths of gullies, knife-edged ridges, and buttes banded in rust, ash gray, and faded cream. A dry wash snakes through the center, its gravel floor scattered with boulders fallen from undercut cliffs. Beyond it rise tilted sedimentary walls and a freestanding mesa with a sheer face catching late sun, while farther back a chain of basalt-capped escarpments closes the horizon. Wind carries fine dust in thin sheets along the ground, emphasizing the depth, scale, and hard geological structure.

Camera push in. A clear mountain lake lies below granite cliffs, its near shoreline edged with rounded stones, submerged branches, and ribbons of green aquatic plants visible through the water. Small wavelets roll in from the open surface, folding around boulders and leaving momentary lace patterns of foam. Across the lake, a narrow waterfall drops from a hanging valley, striking dark rock and bursting into cold spray that drifts over a fan of slick ledges. Reflections of pines and bright cloud fragments tremble and reform with each ripple, while the water shifts from transparent amber near shore to deep steel blue over the submerged drop-off.

Camera pull out. A broad northern marsh at the edge of winter spreads beneath an immense sky, with frozen reeds, black pools, and low peat islands arranged in layered channels that recede toward a distant treeline. The scene sits in the last minutes before sunset as a snow squall passes offshore; one half of the sky remains heavy and slate colored, while the western gap burns with muted gold and rose. Thin ice skins the shallows, reflecting the brighter opening in broken plates, and fresh snow dusts hummocks and driftwood. Flocks of small birds wheel low over the water, and cold haze softens the far horizon as daylight drains away.
